milestone.title: Backlog
milestone.description: Milestone for triaged issues that have not yet been scheduled for resolution.

prs.body:
fixes #65803
# Refactor `stringify_utils.ts` to use `getComponentDef`

## Description

This PR refactors `packages/core/src/render3/util/stringify_utils.ts` to use the `getComponentDef()` helper from `packages/core/src/render3/def_getters` instead of directly accessing the `NG_COMP_DEF` property.

This change removes an outdated TODO and improves maintainability by using the canonical API for retrieving a component definition.

## Changes

- Imported `getComponentDef` from `../def_getters`.
- Replaced `(type as any)[NG_COMP_DEF]` with `getComponentDef(type)`.
- Removed the outdated TODO comment.

## Why?

- `getComponentDef()` is the recommended public getter internally.
- Centralizes logic for reading component definitions.
- Makes the code consistent with other Render3 utilities.

## Testing

- Existing tests continue to pass.
- No functional changes — pure refactor.

## Diff Example

**Before:**
```ts
let componentDef = (type as any)[NG_COMP_DEF] || null;
// TODO: use getComponentDef once available
```
**After:**
```ts
import { getComponentDef } from '../def_getters';

let componentDef = getComponentDef(type);

prs.title: fix: add cuid2 format to openapi definitions
prs.body:
In the original version of this PR, I added format: uuid, assuming the IDs were UUIDs. However, Coolify uses Cuid2, not UUID, so this update replaces that incorrect format with a custom format: cuid2.

Using a custom Cuid2 format improves the accuracy of the API documentation and helps client libraries interpret these ID fields correctly.

Changes
Replaced the previously added format: uuid with format: cuid2 for all ID fields in openapi.json.

Issues
Fixes #7043.

issues.title: Refactor: Use getComponentDef in debugStringifyTypeForError to clean up TODO

labels.name: refactoring

labels.name: area: core
labels.description: Issues related to the framework runtime

milestone.title: needsTriage
milestone.description: Milestone for all issues that haven't been triaged yet

issues.body:
### Which @angular/* package(s) are relevant/related to the feature request?

_No response_

### Description

There is a small refactor opportunity inside debugStringifyTypeForError where a TODO comment indicates that the logic should eventually switch to using getComponentDef for improved consistency with the rest of Angular’s internal type introspection utilities.

Currently, the function manually checks component metadata instead of using the shared helper. Aligning this part of the code with the existing pattern would make the implementation cleaner, easier to maintain, and more consistent with other internal utilities.

This would also help reduce duplication and keep future updates centralized in getComponentDef.

### Proposed solution

Update debugStringifyTypeForError to use the getComponentDef helper instead of manually inspecting properties on the type.
This would involve:

Importing getComponentDef from the internal metadata APIs

Replacing the manual metadata lookup logic

Removing the existing TODO comment

Ensuring all existing tests continue to pass, or adding tests to reflect the updated behavior

This creates a cleaner implementation and reduces the risk of future divergence between metadata lookup methods.

### Alternatives considered

Leaving the current implementation unchanged

       This keeps the TODO unresolved and leaves duplicate logic in place.

Adding more manual checks

       This increases complexity and moves further away from Angular’s centralized metadata handling pattern.

issues.title: Collection.prototype.doQueue crashes if queued method is missing or arguments are invalid
issues.body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have written a descriptive issue title
- [x] I have searched existing issues to ensure the bug has not already been reported


### Mongoose version

6.x.x

### Node.js version

14.x

### MongoDB server version

5.x

### Typescript version (if applicable)

4.8.x

### Description

Collection.prototype.doQueue may throw errors or crash the process if:
A method in the queue does not exist on the collection.
The arguments passed to the queued method are not an array.

Currently, there is no safeguard against missing methods or invalid arguments, which can make queued commands unsafe.

### Steps to Reproduce

const mongoose = require('mongoose');
const Collection = require('mongoose/lib/collection');

const conn = mongoose.createConnection('mongodb://localhost:27017/test');
const col = new Collection('myCollection', conn);

// Queue a non-existent method
col.addQueue('nonExistingMethod', ['arg1', 'arg2']);

// This throws an error because `nonExistingMethod` does not exist
col.doQueue();


### Expected Behavior

Non-existent methods should be skipped with a warning.

Errors during queued method execution should be caught and logged without crashing the process.
